rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY FONG VS. BOLIVAR VALLARINO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Defendant Requests For Admission, Set One, As Admitted By Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, September 20, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT LO CYLEON'S Motion To Deem Defendant Requests For Admission, Set One, As Admitted By Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: (The full tentative ruling has been e-mailed to the parties.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD JOHNSON ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default (Calendared In Dept. 302 In Error. Motion Is Set In Dept. 501 As Indicated On The Papers.)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 20, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTS ANNE KIHAGI, RENKA PROP, LLC's Motion To Set Aside Default. Defendants Anne Kihagi and Renka Prop., LLC's motion to set aside default is granted under CCP §473(b). California's strong public policy favors deciding cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) Defendants' alternate grounds need not be reached. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GARY STAPLETON VS. BURLINGAME POINT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 20, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF GARY STAPLETON's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses. The judge pro tem's well-reasoned recommendation is adopted; plaintiff Gary Stapleton's motion to compel defendants' accident scene photographs is granted. Such photos are not communications, and thus not attorney-client communications. (Suezaki v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 58 Cal.2d 166, 176.) The photographs are arguably attorney work product, but are discoverable under CCP §2018.030(b). Plaintiff could not take his own accident scene photographs, for he was severely injured, and the trench he fell into was filled soon after defendants' photos were taken. The parties have resolved their other discovery disputes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. Please attach a copy of the judge pro tem's report to the order. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GAYLE HALL VS. TRADER JOE'S COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 20, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT TRADER JOE'S COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Trader Joe's Company's demurrers are overruled. The complaint adequately pleads claims under the Unruh Civil Rights and California Disabled Persons acts. A complaint need only provide "notice of the issues sufficient to enable preparation of a defense." (Doe v. City of Los Angeles (2007) 42 Cal.4th 531, 549-550.) Unruh and Disabled Persons claims are not among the few that require particularized pleading. (Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 6-46 et seq.) The few loose references to pleading statutory claims with specificity trace back to suits against public entities, which Trader Joe's is not. (See Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 795.) Whether the fourth cause of action is labelled "negligence" or "negligent infliction of emotional distress" is of no moment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MAPLE MULTI-FAMILY LAND CA, L.P., VS. TERRACON CONSULTANTS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Referee For All Purposes
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 20, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MAPLE MULTI-FAMILY LAND CA, L.P.'s Motion For Order Appointing Referee For All Purposes. Plaintiff Maple Multi-Family Land CA, L.P. filed and served a motion on August 21, 2019, for an order appointing a referee for all purposes. Without excuse or explanation, defendant Terracon Consultants, Inc. filed a tardy opposition on September 19 - seven court days after it was due and two court days after a reply from Maple would have been due. The court desires a non-hurried reply from Maple, so the hearing of this matter is continued to September 27. Maple's reply - it may be of any length - is due on September 25. For their bad-faith actions, Terracon and its counsel are ordered to pay the reasonable expenses, including attorney fees, incurred by Maple to draft, file and argue the reply brief. (CCP §128.5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION A CALIFORNIA VS. ANTHONY VIDEAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 20, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'a MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Defendant Caritas Management Corporation's summary judgment motion is granted. As Caritas notes, plaintiff Anthony Videau's opposing separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350. The document improperly excludes Caritas' evidentiary citations and largely fails to cite opposing evidence to try to create triable issues. This alone is "sufficient ground" to grant the motion. (CCP §437c(b)(3).) But Videau's claims also fail on the merits. Videau signed on-duty meal period agreements on May 18, 2012, August 31, 2017 and September 27, 2017. (UMF 5, 9, 12.) His cryptic reference to Labor Code §226.7(b) on the August 2017 agreement was not a clear revocation of his previous agreement to remain on-duty during meal periods. (See 8 CCR §11050(11)(A).) Had Videau intended to revoke his 2012 consent, he should not have signed the August 2017 agreement, much less the September 2017 agreement. Executing those agreements was conduct directly contrary to revoking his consent. Caritas presents competent evidence that Videau never revoked his consent; Videau adduces no competent controverting evidence, and instead relies on a lawyer's declaration. (UMF 20.) The undisputed evidence establishes that Caritas instructed Videau to take two 15-minute breaks during his shift and he was not required to carry a radio, pager or walkie-talkie. (UMF 23, 24.) Videau argues that the breaks were not meaningful and violated the Labor Code because he was to remain on the premises. No authority supports this notion. Rather, in Augustus v. ABM Security Services, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal.5th 257, 270, the California Supreme Court states: "one would expect that employees will ordinarily have to remain onsite or nearby. This constraint, which is of course common to all rest periods, is not sufficient to establish employer control." Several federal courts have followed Augustus in ruling that employers may require employees to stay onsite during rest breaks. (See, e.g., Ritenour v. Carrington Mortgage Services, LLC (C.D. Cal.) 2018 WL 5858658 at *7 ("The California Supreme Court never stated that an employer cannot require an employee to remain on the premises during a rest period."); Hubbs v. Big Lots Stores, Inc. (C.D. Cal.) 2018 WL 5264141 at *4 ("Augustus…recognized that an employer may require an employee to remain 'on premises' during such rest periods.").) Videau's claims further fail because he adduces attorney argument and a hearing officer's characterizations instead of competent evidence. Videau's claims for waiting time penalties fall with the defective claims already addressed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
